campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met in early September 2025 to review the city's finances for July, including money the city had invested and how much was in the treasury. They also discussed plans to improve how people get around Campbell using different types of transportation, like buses, bikes, and walking paths.
keyTopics: City's investment and cash position for July 2025
Monthly treasurer's financial report
New multimodal transportation plan to improve buses, biking, and pedestrian options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After listening to public input, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city rule) and decided to skip the standard first reading process to move things along faster. The council then scheduled the ordinance to be voted on and officially adopted at their next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on new ordinance
Council waived standard first reading to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for adoption vote at next council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda highlights

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they approved meeting notes from several previous sessions in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details were cut off in the agenda provided.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six previous March meetings
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contract

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review progress on programs serving children and youth in the city. They discussed summer safety initiatives, youth mentorship programs, enforcement of city codes, and plans to improve the library system.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and mentorship programs
Safe Summer Initiative to keep young people engaged during break
Code enforcement activities across the city
Library facility improvements and customer service updates

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in-person and online. Residents could join the virtual meeting and provide public comment on agenda items by using Zoom or calling in by phone.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom or phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
How to raise hand or unmute to speak during the meeting

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular meeting on April 9, 2026, starting at 7 P.M. with the Pledge of Allegiance. Three commissioners shared personal stories about their experiences in Sunnyvale, and the group approved the meeting notes from their previous gathering in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission regular meeting
Commissioner stories and community perspectives
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Public hearings and general business items

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs and initiatives. The main topics included a presentation about a food-related program called Budget and Bites, a youth organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for young people
Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 11
Public comment period for community members

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using a hybrid format that allowed people to attend in person or join online. The council provided multiple ways for residents to participate remotely and submit their feedback on agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote via Zoom)
Public comment submission process through eComments
Remote participation options for residents unable to attend in person

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and a review of new project requests from individual council members. The council also considered changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled and reviewed their upcoming meeting schedule.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's federal housing and community development funding plan
New project requests submitted by council members
Changes to how council fills vacant seats
Approval of previous meeting minutes and next meeting agenda preview

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed and approved the gas utility's financial plan for the next fiscal year, which included adjusting how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service. The council also approved reserve transfers and changes to the gas rate schedules for different types of customers (residential, commercial, and large commercial users).
keyTopics: Gas utility rates will change for residents and businesses in FY 2027
City is transferring money between the general fund and gas utility reserves
Three different rate schedules updated (residential, commercial, large commercial)
Meeting lasted about one hour (6:35 PM - 7:35 PM)
